extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from 4-rider breakaway that held minute gap after peloton fragmented in horrific weather, numerous crashes, and clothing changes disrupted sprint team organization.
tactical_patterns: Clothing changes during horrific weather disrupted chase organization; Gaviria crashed immediately after changing, never regained contact with peloton.
Breakaway survived because sprinter teams fractured during chaotic conditions; Cavendish regained contact but without full leadout train operational.
Ineos strategically protected four riders into top-10 after Vlasov/Evenepoel COVID exits, positioning depth advantage for mountain stages.

rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: negative: Abandoned feeling ill only 2 hours into stage after rest day, suggesting vulnerability to illness beyond just COVID cases.
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Lost contact with maglia rosa group on transitional stage, dropped 10 minutes and out of top-10, revealing fragility on non-mountain terrain.
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: Won intermediate sprint over Matthews then attacked from peloton with Caruso, showing versatility beyond pure sprinting.
rider_signals.GEE Derek: positive: Second podium finish at Giro after launching first decisive attack inside 1500m, establishing breakaway acceleration threat.
rider_signals.DUNBAR Eddie: positive: Moved into top-9 GC as Ineos fourth rider in top-10, benefiting from chaos without explicit aggressive riding.
